title: How A Bill Becomes A Law

jokes.text: Yes. I am big enough to admit that I am often inspired by myself.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing her wall of inspirational women and pointing to a picture
jokes.punchline: The picture is of herself, delivered with complete sincerity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her earnest self-regard delivered without irony. The phrase 'big enough to admit' makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: Occupied!
jokes.setup: Leslie proudly showing off her private bathroom
jokes.punchline: Someone is already using it
jokes.explanation: Decent setup/payoff for bathroom conflict storyline. Simple but effective puncturing of Leslie's pride.

jokes.text: I think it is crazy that the most junior city councilor gets a private bathroom just because she is a girl.
jokes.setup: Jamm explaining why he's using Leslie's bathroom
jokes.punchline: Reveals his sexist reasoning
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jamm's character as petty and sexist. Not particularly clever but serves the story.

jokes.text: I'm going to drop my liquids in here, take my solids down the hall. Deal?
jokes.setup: Jamm proposing a bathroom-sharing solution
jokes.punchline: Grotesquely clinical description of his bathroom habits
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that fits Jamm's character. 'Liquids' and 'solids' euphemisms are mildly clever.

jokes.text: How?
jokes.setup: Jamm's gross bathroom proposal
jokes.punchline: Leslie's stunned, single-word response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - one word captures Leslie's bewilderment at Jamm's logic.

jokes.text: You can't share too much or too often.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining his therapy oversharing
jokes.punchline: Quoting advice that contradicts social norms
jokes.explanation: Classic Chris moment - taking therapeutic advice too literally. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: That's too much, Jerry. For real. Keep us out your bedroom.
jokes.setup: Jerry mentioning couples therapy
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately shutting down oversharing
jokes.explanation: Standard Tom being dismissive of Jerry's personal life. Functional but not memorable.

jokes.text: Thank you, Andy. I agree.
jokes.setup: Chris presenting the 311 idea
jokes.punchline: Thanking Andy for agreeing with Chris's own idea
jokes.explanation: Good Chris character moment - his earnest confusion about social interaction.

jokes.text: You're welcome, Chris.
jokes.setup: Chris thanking himself
jokes.punchline: Now he's welcoming his own thanks
jokes.explanation: Escalates the previous joke perfectly. Chris having a full conversation with himself is peak character comedy.

jokes.text: I sound insane. I'm going to go talk to my therapist.
jokes.setup: Chris recognizing he sounds crazy
jokes.punchline: His solution is more therapy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris logic - recognizing the problem but doubling down on what caused it.

jokes.text: Well, that's your opinion.
jokes.setup: Leslie correcting Joan that something is opinion, not fact
jokes.punchline: Joan treats the definition of opinion as just another opinion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant circular logic joke. Joan's intellectual stubbornness creates perfect absurdist moment.

jokes.text: Because not only am I a city councilor. I'm a city councilor with porpoise.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why she'll help the swim team
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'purpose' using the team mascot
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie pun delivered with complete earnestness. Not groundbreaking but fits her character perfectly.

jokes.text: We came in fourth in the County swim meet last year.
jokes.setup: Joan asking about their greatest accomplishment
jokes.punchline: Proudly stating they came in fourth
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from kids' earnest mediocrity. Sets up the contrast with Leslie's grand rhetoric.

jokes.text: Porpoise call! [awkward silence]
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to connect with the swim team
jokes.punchline: Nobody responds to her attempted team call
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Leslie's desperation to connect falling flat is painfully relatable.

jokes.text: I'm not weird. Okay, everybody leave.
jokes.setup: The awkward porpoise call moment
jokes.punchline: Leslie's defensive overreaction
jokes.explanation: Leslie's attempt to maintain dignity after embarrassment. Good character moment.

jokes.text: And writing Star Trek fan fiction does not count.
jokes.setup: April asking Ben if he's busy
jokes.punchline: Preemptively dismissing his nerdy hobby
jokes.explanation: Perfect April knowing Ben well enough to predict his excuse. Good character relationship humor.

jokes.text: I... finished it last week.
jokes.setup: April's dismissal of Star Trek fan fiction
jokes.punchline: Ben admitting he was actually doing exactly that
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to April's setup. Ben's sheepish admission is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Dude, 30 years from now, when you're on your deathbed, what are you going to remember? Organizing files or taking a spontaneous road trip to surprise Leslie?
jokes.setup: Ben hesitating about the road trip
jokes.punchline: April giving unexpectedly wise life advice
jokes.explanation: Funny because April rarely gives sincere advice. The deathbed perspective is unexpectedly profound from her.

jokes.text: We'll jam out to some sweet tunage.
jokes.setup: Ben getting excited about the road trip
jokes.punchline: Using awkward slang word 'tunage'
jokes.explanation: Ben trying to be cool and failing. 'Tunage' is a nicely awkward word choice for his character.

jokes.text: Actually, no. I don't want to go any more. / Just kidding. / We're not going. / Road trip! / So we're going? / Yes!
jokes.setup: Ben's enthusiasm for the trip
jokes.punchline: April jerking him around with rapid reversals
jokes.explanation: Perfect April chaos. The rapid emotional whiplash is exactly her MO for messing with people.

jokes.text: She's like the little sister I never had. Because the little sister I do have is normal and not terrifying.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining his relationship with April
jokes.punchline: Contrasting her with his normal sister
jokes.explanation: Nice structure with the setup/reversal. 'Normal and not terrifying' is a good character description for April.

jokes.text: Government is inefficient and should be dissolved. Please hold while I transfer you.
jokes.setup: Ron working the 311 line
jokes.punchline: Giving anti-government speech while doing government work
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson. The juxtaposition of his philosophy with his actions is perfectly executed.

jokes.text: Well, how do I get I.T. Here? / Call 311.
jokes.setup: Ron needs I.T. help with the 311 system
jokes.punchline: Donna tells him to call the system that's broken
jokes.explanation: Good circular bureaucracy joke. The economy of the exchange makes it work well.

jokes.text: Andrew, get your lunch, some water, and a 40-pound bag of asphalt. / Hey!
jokes.setup: Ron listing typical work supplies
jokes.punchline: Unexpectedly including heavy construction materials
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from normal items to absurd ones. Andy's enthusiasm makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No, no. This is 311. / Donna, they switched my phone with 911.
jokes.setup: The 311 system problems continue
jokes.punchline: Now it's connected to emergency services
jokes.explanation: Escalates the bureaucratic incompetence theme. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Living in this town's like living in the devil's butt crack.
jokes.setup: Complaining about the heat
jokes.punchline: Colorful metaphor for how hot it is
jokes.explanation: Vivid imagery that fits Donna's character. Not groundbreaking but effective.

jokes.text: My God, that is so interesting to me because I'm a map salesman.
jokes.setup: Ben talking about GPS routes
jokes.punchline: April's sarcastic fake enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect April sarcasm. The fake profession adds to the absurdity of her mock interest.

jokes.text: Why aren't we moving? / Because. Other cars.
jokes.setup: Ben asking about traffic
jokes.punchline: April's overly literal explanation
jokes.explanation: April's characteristically unhelpful response. The economy of 'Other cars' is perfect.

jokes.text: It's because the two leading industries here are corn syrup and rubber nipples. It is a dentistry jackpot.
jokes.setup: Jamm explaining why he moved to Pawnee
jokes.punchline: Targeting industries that would ruin teeth
jokes.explanation: Clever cynical logic that fits Jamm perfectly. 'Rubber nipples' is an unexpected detail that makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, D.D.S. Doesn't stand for 'dumb, dumb, stupid.'
jokes.setup: Discussing dentistry
jokes.punchline: Jamm's defensive play on his degree
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that shows Jamm's insecurity about his profession. Adequate but not memorable.

jokes.text: Not my initials, but thank you so much.
jokes.setup: Tom showing his personalized cigar cutter
jokes.punchline: Jamm noting it's not actually for him
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Jamm's polite but pointed observation. Works but isn't special.

jokes.text: So when the Ann's away, the mice get perms.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why she's getting a perm
jokes.punchline: Mangling the 'cat's away' saying
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie malapropism. The perm/play connection is cute and fits her character.

jokes.text: The perm must wait, Autumn! The perm must wait.
jokes.setup: Leslie having to abandon her perm for politics
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration about perm postponement
jokes.explanation: Leslie's priorities in action. The dramatic delivery of something so trivial is perfect for her character.

jokes.text: Yeah, I know how to do math, Mason.
jokes.setup: Kid pointing out they don't have enough votes
jokes.punchline: Leslie snapping at a child
jokes.explanation: Funny because Leslie is being defensive to a literal child. Shows her stress level perfectly.

jokes.text: You know, screw Jamm. Screw Jamm.
jokes.setup: Leslie's frustration building
jokes.punchline: Repeating her mild profanity for emphasis
jokes.explanation: Leslie trying to be tough but still using mild language. The repetition shows her building anger.

jokes.text: I don't know how to answer that.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking how she looks mid-perm disaster
jokes.punchline: Tom's diplomatic non-answer
jokes.explanation: Tom's tactful avoidance of commenting on Leslie's appearance. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: we do humbly request thine permission to befix doth holes yonder.
jokes.setup: Kids demanding he ask permission to fix the pothole
jokes.punchline: Andy speaking in mock medieval language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - going along with kids' fantasy with full commitment. 'Befix' is a nice made-up word.

jokes.text: Well, uh, I kind of look at it like it's your favorite directors making a mix tape just for you.
jokes.setup: April asking about his soundtrack collection
jokes.punchline: Ben's overly romantic view of movie soundtracks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerdiness - overthinking something simple. The romantic language is endearingly awkward.

jokes.text: Benji's Cool Times Summer Jamz Mix
jokes.setup: Ben having embarrassing personal mixes
jokes.punchline: The awkward title of his mix
jokes.explanation: 'Benji' and the Z in 'Jamz' are perfectly embarrassing choices. Good character detail.

jokes.text: Don't judge me.
jokes.setup: The terrible music starting to play
jokes.punchline: Ben's pre-emptive defense
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective embarrassment. Ben knows how bad this is going to be.

jokes.text: I've eaten the same lunch for 58 years. Caesar salad, extra dressing, extra croutons, and extra anchovies.
jokes.setup: Milton refusing to rush his lunch
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and long-term lunch routine
jokes.explanation: Good old-person stubbornness. 58 years of identical lunches is absurd but believable for his character.

jokes.text: Their platform? 'De-integrate Baseball.'
jokes.setup: Explaining Milton's political history
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific racist platform
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about historical racism. 'De-integrate' as a political platform is absurdly specific.

jokes.text: You can really taste the ignorance. / It's pronounced 'anchovies.'
jokes.setup: Tom making a joke about the racist salad
jokes.punchline: Milton missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Brilliant joke structure. Tom's clever wordplay completely whooshes past Milton, who corrects the wrong thing.

jokes.text: Seaweed choke! / That large boy is my colleague.
jokes.setup: Andy apparently choking or fighting kids
jokes.punchline: Ron's matter-of-fact introduction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan. Calling Andy a 'large boy' while he's apparently in combat is perfectly Ron.

jokes.text: Tim, Mary, Jack, uh, Tim, Marie, Tim, and Mary. / Don't forget about Tim. / Right, Tim. / You know, it's very hard to tell.
jokes.setup: Milton listing his grandchildren
jokes.punchline: Multiple kids with same names, can't keep count
jokes.explanation: Good escalating confusion. Multiple Tims and Marys suggests old-fashioned naming and poor memory.

jokes.text: What country is he from? / Iceland.
jokes.setup: Milton looking at Tom
jokes.punchline: Random guess about Tom's ethnicity
jokes.explanation: Milton's casual racism/confusion. Iceland is an absurdly wrong guess for Tom's background.

jokes.text: Data had never felt this way before. Of course, data had never felt anything before. But Captain Picard couldn't help but note the smile that crept over his mechanical but lifelike face.
jokes.setup: Ben reading his Star Trek fan fiction
jokes.punchline: Overly romantic description of android Data
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben nerd cringe. The overly earnest romantic language about an android is beautifully awkward.

jokes.text: I'm gonna murder you.
jokes.setup: Ben's terrible fan fiction
jokes.punchline: April's understated threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deadpan response. The casual death threat is exactly her style of humor.

jokes.text: Right? / So, how exactly are you gonna fix this hole? Just for my own edification.
jokes.setup: Ron and Diane bonding over Food and Stuff
jokes.punchline: Diane using formal language for a simple question
jokes.explanation: 'For my own edification' is unnecessarily fancy language that suggests education/intelligence. Good character building.

jokes.text: ♪ Ron and Diane sitting in a tree k-i-s-s-i-s-s-i-p-p-i.
jokes.setup: Ron and Diane getting along
jokes.punchline: Andy singing but getting confused with spelling
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy confusion. Starting to spell 'kissing' but switching to 'Mississippi' is perfectly him.

jokes.text: I've got Irritable Bowel Syndrome.
jokes.setup: Jamm explaining why he needs the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Oversharing medical information
jokes.explanation: Fits Jamm's character as someone who overshares gross details. Not particularly clever but effective.

jokes.text: you can put your butt back in your pants, sir.
jokes.setup: Leslie rejecting Jamm's bathroom demands
jokes.punchline: Telling him to contain his bathroom issues
jokes.explanation: Leslie trying to be tough while staying polite. 'Put your butt back in your pants' is a unique insult.

jokes.text: Or does it require two princesses?
jokes.setup: Ron refusing to dress up, Andy needing help
jokes.punchline: Andy suggesting they both become princesses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - if one princess is good, two must be better. The image of Ron as a princess is funny.

jokes.text: You remind me of a young, beautiful Strom Thurmond.
jokes.setup: Milton complimenting Leslie
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to a notorious racist politician
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly uncomfortable compliment. Milton thinking this is flattering reveals his complete lack of awareness about history.

jokes.text: She tried to kiss me. / She was insatiable.
jokes.setup: Milton claiming Leslie was aggressive
jokes.punchline: Escalating to dramatic language about her 'insatiable' nature
jokes.explanation: 'Insatiable' is perfectly over-the-top for Milton's dramatic interpretation of Leslie's desperation.

jokes.text: You're missing the backs of your upper canines. Sloppy technique.
jokes.setup: Jamm offering a toothbrush
jokes.punchline: Professional critique of Tom's dental hygiene
jokes.explanation: Perfect dentist behavior - can't help but diagnose dental problems. The technical language makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I do not negotiate with dentists.
jokes.setup: Pressure to compromise with Jamm
jokes.punchline: Leslie taking a principled stand against dental professionals
jokes.explanation: Fantastic parody of 'we don't negotiate with terrorists.' Leslie treating dentists as an enemy class is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: I'm sorry, it's just... your face is ridiculous.
jokes.setup: Ron dressed as a princess
jokes.punchline: Diane's blunt assessment of his appearance
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective. Diane's directness matches Ron's personality while acknowledging the visual absurdity.

jokes.text: We're out of gas.
jokes.setup: Finally getting traffic moving
jokes.punchline: Immediately hitting another obstacle
jokes.explanation: Classic comedy structure - moment of hope immediately dashed. Simple but effective timing.

jokes.text: Can't wait to drop my kids at the pool. By 'kids,' I mean turds.
jokes.setup: Jamm getting the bathroom he wanted
jokes.punchline: Explaining his euphemism unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Crude humor that fits Jamm's character but isn't particularly clever. The explanation ruins the euphemism.

jokes.text: Lay the mother on her side and try to move the baby in a corkscrew fashion.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about difficult birth positions
jokes.punchline: Donna casually having expert medical knowledge
jokes.explanation: Reveals Donna's unexpected expertise. The specific medical advice contrasts with her usual disinterest in work.

jokes.text: 'A,' I don't recall inviting you in here, and 'B,' I did not like her.
jokes.setup: Andy commenting on Ron liking Diane
jokes.punchline: Ron's formal denial structure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron formality when flustered. The A/B structure shows his attempt to maintain control.

jokes.text: See how it feels?
jokes.setup: April jerking Ben around about weekend plans
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was intentional payback
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that reveals April's emotional intelligence. She was teaching Ben a lesson about jerking people around.

jokes.text: Is that a euphemism? / No. / Then great work.
jokes.setup: Ron saying he 'fixed her pothole'
jokes.punchline: Chris's relief that it wasn't sexual
jokes.explanation: Good double entendre joke. Chris's innocent confusion about whether it's sexual innuendo is funny.

jokes.text: I'm a middle school vice-principal. I don't screw around. Does that freak you out?
jokes.setup: Diane asking Ron out
jokes.punchline: Her direct, no-nonsense approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect match for Ron's personality. Her directness and professional authority would appeal to him.

jokes.text: No need to wear makeup.
jokes.setup: Ron accepting the dinner date
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at a compliment
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron attempt at romance. Well-meaning but socially awkward in exactly his way.

jokes.text: Because it's fun in the sun!
jokes.setup: Jamm about to reveal Leslie's compromise
jokes.punchline: Getting pushed into the pool mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy timing. Jamm getting interrupted by the pool he was criticizing is poetic justice.

jokes.text: No, my perm.
jokes.setup: Everyone jumping in the pool
jokes.punchline: Leslie's horror at her hair getting wet
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the perm storyline. Leslie's vanity vs. her political success creates good conflict.

jokes.text: And the Tommy Haverford member's chair.
jokes.setup: Tom showing off his new cigar club
jokes.punchline: Having a chair specifically named after himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom narcissism. Getting his own named chair in a club he just created is exactly his style.

jokes.text: I do not like this.
jokes.setup: Tom's elaborate cigar club celebration
jokes.punchline: Ron's simple, understated disapproval
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron economy. His simple disapproval of Tom's flashy celebration is exactly right for his character.

tmdb_overview: Leslie helps a local swim team get more practice time by working on a new law that extends the hours at the community pool; Ron finds himself filling a pothole for a local woman (Lucy Lawless) as part of Chris' new plan to solve citizens' problems around the city; April and Ben take a road trip to Pawnee.
insight: Deadpan delivery drives 70 jokes across legislative satire with modest 2.83-per-minute density.
